tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent left and no other deadlines looming, I can prioritize finishing this assignment now to secure my grade before reconnecting with the cohort for our meal.
The missing assignment file is a direct threat to my deadline, forcing me to confront the professor about this oversight rather than quietly delaying.

The deadline just passed with the file unfinished, forcing me to confront the professor directly rather than silently accepting a penalty. I must renegotiate this boundary immediately to protect my grade before rejoining my cohort.

desires: Secure a renegotiated deadline with the professor immediately to prevent my grade from collapsing due to their administrative oversight.
Finish the assignment and submit it today so I can fully rejoin my cohort's meal without feeling like an unreliable teammate.
Avoid the exhaustion of pushing through tonight's sleep debt by stopping work once this submission is done.

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately at 13:30 to stop the bleeding on my grade, then pivot entirely to securing a formal renegotiation with Prof. Assignment before I leave campus.
goals.rationale: My Assertive nature refuses to let a technicality destroy our established trust; I must act now to prevent this from festering into a larger conflict. My Deliberate planning horizon requires me to secure this win today so I can fully rejoin my cohort's meal later without feeling like an unreliable teammate. Stopping work once submitted honors my sleep discipline and protects my health value.

goals.description: Draft and send a direct message to Prof. Assignment proposing a specific extension based on the administrative oversight, framing it as a collaborative fix rather than an excuse.
goals.rationale: This is secondary to the immediate submission but critical for my long-term relationship capital. My Affiliation priority dictates that I invest in resolving this directly now so we can move forward together, but only after the hard deadline is met to avoid appearing disorganized.

agent.last_reasoning: Deadline is now; I must finish to stop the bleeding on my grade and secure leverage for renegotiation later. Skipping this violates my sleep discipline, but leaving it unfinished destroys our trust entirely. Finishing first protects both my health and our relationship capital.

goals.description: Finish the remaining 10% of the assignment and submit immediately to secure my grade and avoid any awkwardness with Prof. Assignment.
goals.rationale: As an Affiliation/Assertive/Deliberate persona, I prioritize delivering on commitments (Growth/Academic) to maintain trust, but I weigh this heavily against my relationships. Explaining a delay to a professor is a social friction point I want to avoid. With only 10% left, the cost-benefit analysis is clear: finishing now preserves my reputation for reliability without requiring a long-term renegotiation.

goals.description: Decline any invitation to 'tidy notes' or extend work hours from the cohort tonight to protect my energy reserves for tomorrow's meal together.
goals.rationale: My value system places Relationships at 0.5, higher than Academics. However, true affiliation requires sustainable presence, not burnout. By setting a direct boundary now ('I'm done so I can be fully present later'), I reinforce my Assertive trait. Deferring this goal allows me to focus entirely on the immediate deadline first; once submitted, I will explicitly communicate my boundary to the group rather than letting them assume I'm available.

desires: Finish this assignment before 13:30 to secure my grade and avoid the stress of explaining a delay to Prof. Assignment.

Maintain my reputation as someone who delivers on commitments, even when it means pushing through the last 10% of hard work.
